item.title: 中国在交易热潮后收紧通往香港IPO的关键渠道
item.description: 据知情人士透露，北京方面正在限制在海外注册的中国企业赴香港进行首次公开募股，此举可能颠覆这一延续数十年的惯例——该惯例曾推动了数十亿美元的股票销售。【原文：Beijing is restricting Chinese companies incorporated overseas from seeking initial public offerings in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter, threatening to upend a decades-old playbook that has fueled billions of dollars in share sales.】

item.title: 特朗普推迟与习近平的峰会，为中国争取了时间来谋划对伊战争
item.description: 唐纳德·特朗普总统要求推迟与中方领导人习近平的峰会，这一举动对北京方面而言或许是个好消息，尽管此举可能给世界两大经济体之间的关系带来新的不确定性。【原文：President Donald Trump’s request for a delay to his summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping is a likely welcome development for Beijing, even as it threatens to inject new uncertainty into ties between the world’s two largest economies.】

item.title: 据称，金融科技公司Upvest在融资中估值达6.4亿欧元
item.description: 德国金融科技公司Upvest（为Revolut等数字银行及零售贷款机构提供经纪技术）在最新一轮融资中筹集了1.25亿美元，现有投资者也参与了本轮融资。【原文：German fintech Upvest, which supplies brokerage technology to neobanks such as Revolut and to retail lenders, raised $125 million in a new funding round that included its existing backers.】

item.title: 尽管政府反对，瑞士人口上限提案仍获得45%的支持率
item.description: 尽管政界人士和商界领袖多次对此表示反对，瑞士右翼人民党提出的将该国人口上限设定为1000万的计划仍获得公众的强烈支持。【原文：A plan by Switzerland’s right-wing People’s Party to cap the country’s population at 10 million still has strong support from the public despite repeated pushback by politicians and business leaders against such a move.】

item.title: 摩根士丹利称南非将维持长期按兵不动
item.description: 摩根士丹利表示，中东冲突引发的油价飙升可能会推高南非通胀率，并导致政策制定者在未来数月内维持利率不变。【原文：Surging oil prices triggered by the Middle East conflict is likely to push South African inflation higher and see policymakers keep interest rates unchanged for months, according to Morgan Stanley.】

item.title: 加拿大亿万富翁史密斯收购《经济学人》杂志26.9%的股份
item.description: 据相关公司透露，加拿大亿万富翁斯蒂芬·史密斯及其家族控股公司已达成协议，将收购《经济学人》杂志出版商的少数股权。【原文：Canadian billionaire Stephen Smith and his family holding company have reached an agreement to acquire a minority stake in the publisher of The Economist magazine, according to the companies.】

item.title: 央行行长们能让伊朗这头“熊”安分下来吗？
item.description:
本周召开会议的18家央行，最初的议程中并没有“战争”这一项。【原文：

The 18 central banks meeting this week didn’t initially have a war on their agendas.

】

item.title: 菲律宾财政部长称油价冲击或将促使4月加息
item.description: 据财政部长弗雷德里克·戈称，如果油价继续上涨，菲律宾央行下个月可能会收紧货币政策。【原文：The Philippine central bank may tighten its monetary policy next month if oil prices continue to rise, according to Finance Secretary Frederick Go.】

item.title: 印度UTI养老基金在经历了一轮股票投资热潮后转向债券
item.description: 在经历了一年大规模买入股票之后，印度第三大养老基金正重返债券市场，这一转变或将为该国饱受重创的债券市场带来一些缓解。【原文：India’s third-largest pension fund is returning to bonds after a year of heavy equity buying, a shift that could bring some relief to the country’s battered debt market.】

item.title: 日本20年期国债发行需求强劲，收益率走高
item.description: 周二，日本20年期国债拍卖需求强劲，因收益率上升吸引了买家，这缓解了市场对油价上涨和通胀风险可能抑制投资意愿的担忧。【原文：Japan’s 20-year government bond auction on Tuesday drew solid demand as higher yields attracted buyers, easing concerns that rising oil prices and inflation risks would damp appetite.】

item.title: 高盛称，原油市场的最大冲击将对炼油产品造成最严重打击
item.description: 高盛集团表示，中东战争引发的史上最大规模石油市场震荡，对航空燃油和柴油等成品油的影响将大于对原油的影响。【原文：The largest oil market shock on record triggered by the war in the Middle East is set to have a greater impact on products such as jet fuel and diesel than on crude, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.】

item.title: 澳大利亚央行在分歧中连续两次加息
item.description: 澳大利亚央行周二连续第二次上调基准利率，以加大对顽固通胀的打击力度，因为伊朗局势升级导致的能源成本上涨，可能加剧物价上涨压力。【原文：Australia’s central bank raised its key interest rate for a second straight meeting on Tuesday, stepping up its battle against stubborn inflation as rising energy costs from the widening war in Iran threaten to intensify price pressures.】

item.title: 随着交易员为中东地区可能出现的更多停电做准备，铝价反弹
item.description: 铝价在连续两日下跌后回升，因伊朗战争持续时间的不确定性加剧了市场对中东地区主要冶炼厂可能进一步减产的担忧。【原文：Aluminum recovered after a two-day slide, as uncertainty over the duration of the Iran war fueled worries about further potential output cuts at major plants across the Middle East.】

item.title: 随着油价持续坚挺，印度企业界正为盈利承压做准备
item.description: 野村证券预计Nifty指数年底将收于24,900点。【原文：Nomura sees the Nifty at 24,900 at year-end.】

item.title: 峰会前夕，日本对特朗普提出的舰船要求保持谨慎态度
item.description: 日本防卫大臣表示，美国并未提出向中东派遣军舰的具体要求。此前有媒体报道称，在首相高市早苗本周与美国总统唐纳德·特朗普举行峰会之前，东京方面已开始探讨这一可能性。【原文：Japan’s defense minister said the US hadn’t made a specific demand to send ships to the Middle East, following media reports that Tokyo has started to explore that possibility ahead of Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s summit with US President Donald Trump this week.】

item.title: SK董事长表示，内存芯片短缺将持续至2030年
item.description: 韩国SK集团总裁表示，由于半导体生产中存在系统性瓶颈，全球内存芯片短缺的情况可能会再持续四到五年。【原文：A global shortage of memory chips is likely to persist another four to five years because of endemic constraints in semiconductor production, the head of South Korean conglomerate SK Group said.】

item.title: 印度资深银行家表示，随着存款量萎缩，亟需新工具
item.description: 印度各家银行正竭力说服偏爱股票的投资者将资金存入存款账户。资深银行家尼拉杰·甘比尔表示，如果银行能有更多筹资渠道，情况会有所改善。【原文：Indian lenders are struggling to convince equity-loving investors to save their money in deposits. Veteran banker Neeraj Gambhir says it would be helpful if banks had more options to raise funds.】

item.title: 油价飙升令日本央行担忧通胀过热风险
item.description: 对于大多数经济学家而言，日本央行本周维持政策不变已是板上钉钉，但此次会议仍具有重大意义，因为当局正开始权衡飙升的油价是否需要采取预防性措施，以防止通胀过热。【原文：A policy hold by the Bank of Japan this week is a foregone conclusion for most economists, but the deliberations will still be highly significant as authorities start to mull whether surging oil prices might warrant preemptive action to keep inflation from overheating.】

item.title: 特朗普加大施压之际，鲁比奥敦促韩国支持霍尔木兹海峡行动
item.description: 美国国务卿马可·鲁比奥敦促作为主要石油进口国的韩国协助重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，此举进一步强化了唐纳德·特朗普总统旨在说服安全盟友协助美国保障这条关键水道安全的努力。【原文：US Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged South Korea, a major oil importer, to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, doubling down on President Donald Trump’s drive to coax security allies into helping America secure the key waterway.】

item.title: 日本官员片山表示，外汇走势与基本面不符
item.description: 日本财务大臣表示，近期汇率的波动与基本面不符，并重申当局可能采取行动加以应对。【原文：Japan’s finance minister said recent currency moves are not in line with fundamentals, reiterating warnings of possible action by authorities to counter the moves.】

item.title: 中国铝业吸引因战争而转移的原材料
item.description: 中国铝生产商有望从中东地区原材料供应路线的调整中受益。【原文：Chinese aluminum producers are set to benefit from a rerouting of raw materials away from the Middle East.】

item.title: 英国将投资10亿英镑用于量子计算研究与试验
item.description: 未来四年，英国将在量子计算研究领域投入超过10亿英镑（13亿美元），各国政府正加大对这项技术的投资力度，因为该技术被越来越视为国家安全的关键所在。【原文：The UK will spend more than £1 billion ($1.3 billion) on quantum computing research over the next four years, as governments boost investment into a technology that’s increasingly considered critical to national security.】

item.title: 随着伊朗战争引发亚洲资产市场震荡，全球基金将目光投向马来西亚
item.description: 早在伊朗战争引发能源价格飙升之前，马来西亚就已从东南亚各国中脱颖而出，成为全球投资者眼中的新宠。【原文：Even before the war in Iran sparked a surge in energy prices, Malaysia stood out from its Southeast Asian peers as the newfound darling of global investors.】

item.title: 摩根士丹利称，私人信贷违约率将升至8%
item.description: 摩根士丹利表示，随着人工智能的进步继续颠覆软件行业，直接贷款的违约率将攀升至8%。【原文：Default rates in direct lending will climb to 8% as advances in artificial intelligence continue to disrupt the software industry, according to Morgan Stanley.】

item.title: 美元走软，交易员权衡石油供应短缺问题，金价持稳
item.description: 受美元走软以及交易员权衡各方为缓解中东战争引发的石油供应冲击所作努力的影响，金价基本持平。【原文：Gold was little changed, after the US dollar slipped and traders weighed attempts to contain an oil-supply shock arising from the war in the Middle East.】

item.title: 里夫斯在英国增长愿景中承诺实现七国集团中最快的AI应用
item.description: 蕾切尔·里夫斯承诺，英国将比其他任何七国集团国家更快地采用人工智能技术，她称这项技术是其推动经济增长计划的关键支柱。【原文：Rachel Reeves vowed the UK will adopt artificial intelligence faster than any other Group of Seven nation, as she hailed the technology as a key plank of her bid to spur economic growth.】

item.title: 油价回落提振市场情绪，亚股料将上涨：市场综述
item.description: 随着油价回落提振华尔街股市，加之市场寄望于将有更多油轮能够通过霍尔木兹海峡，亚洲股市有望上涨。【原文：Stocks in Asia were poised to rise after a pullback in oil prices lifted Wall Street, amid hopes that more tankers will be able to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.】

item.title: 油价反弹，投资者正评估伊朗战争对供应的影响
item.description: 周一油价下跌逾5%后，周二出现反弹，投资者一方面评估中东地区供应面临的威胁，另一方面关注原油供应可能增加的迹象。【原文：Oil rose after slipping more than 5% on Monday, as investors assessed threats to supplies in the Middle East against signs that more crude may become available.】

item.title: 印尼料将维持利率不变，因战争及财政风险令印尼盾承压
item.description: 鉴于中东战事以及投资者对财政纪律的担忧给印尼盾带来日益增大的压力，印尼央行预计将维持基准利率不变。【原文：Indonesia’s central bank is expected to keep its key interest rate unchanged as policymakers confront mounting pressure on the rupiah from war in the Middle East and investor concerns over fiscal discipline.】

item.title: 尽管油价飙升，摩根士丹利仍维持6月降息的预测
item.description: 尽管油价飙升促使交易员们收敛了对政策制定者今年将降息幅度的押注，摩根士丹利仍维持其预测，认为美联储将于6月重启降息，并在9月再次降息。【原文：Morgan Stanley is sticking with a forecast that sees the Federal Reserve resuming interest rates cuts in June and delivering another reduction in September, even as soaring oil prices prompt traders to curb bets for how much policymakers will lower borrowing costs this year.】
